highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.178 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a powerful new `Tool(param:value)` syntax for permission rules, letting you control which tools Claude can use based on their input parameters — for example, blocking Opus-powered subagents with `Agent(model:opus)`. It also improves skill loading so that skills in nested `.claude/skills` directories activate automatically when you're working in those folders. These changes give teams finer-grained control over automated workflows and project-specific customization.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.176 Released
highlights.summary: Session titles are now generated in the language of your conversation, making it easier to stay organized if you work in multiple languages — you can pin a specific language via the new `language` setting. This release also adds `footerLinksRegexes` for custom link badges in the footer and improves Bedrock credential handling. If you work with AWS Bedrock or on multilingual projects, this update is worth grabbing.

highlights.title: Anthropic Pauses Credit Change for Claude Code
highlights.summary: Anthropic has reversed a planned billing change that would have moved Claude Agent SDK, `claude -p`, and third-party Agent SDK apps off subscription rate limits onto a separate monthly credit pool. This pause is good news for developers who rely on these features within their existing subscription, as their workflows won't be disrupted for now. Keep an eye on official communications for when this change is eventually rescheduled.

highlights.title: Show HN: Claude Code for Visual Studio (Native Diff with Accept/Reject)
highlights.summary: A community developer has built an unofficial Claude Code extension for Visual Studio (the full IDE, not VS Code), filling a gap since Anthropic only ships official plugins for VS Code and JetBrains. It implements the same protocol as the official plugins, giving you native diff views with accept/reject controls right inside Visual Studio. If you're a C++ or .NET developer stuck on Visual Studio, this is worth trying.

highlights.title: Show HN: macOS Menu Bar Gauges for Your Claude Code Quota
highlights.summary: This open-source macOS tool puts a live usage gauge for your Claude Code quota directly in the menu bar, so you can monitor consumption at a glance without switching contexts. It's especially handy for power users and teams on capped plans who want to avoid unexpected rate-limit surprises mid-session. Install it from the linked GitHub repo — it requires minimal setup.

highlights.title: From Zero to Claude Code in 19 Minutes (No Code)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks complete beginners through setting up and using Claude Code from scratch in under 20 minutes, with no prior coding experience required. It's a fast, practical starting point if you want to understand what Claude Code can do before diving into more technical guides. Great first watch before exploring the longer beginner courses.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This concise YouTube video covers Claude Code from basic usage all the way through to advanced features in a single 20-minute session, making it ideal for developers who want a structured overview without committing to a full course. It's well-suited for anyone who has already installed Claude Code and wants to level up their workflow quickly. Watch this after the zero-to-setup videos to start applying more powerful techniques.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `Tool(param:value)` permission syntax in your `.claude/settings.json` to lock down which models subagents can use — for example, add `Agent(model:opus)` to your deny list on cost-sensitive projects to prevent accidental Opus usage in automated pipelines.

If you work across multiple languages or collaborate internationally, set the `language` setting in Claude Code to pin session titles and responses to your preferred language, keeping your session history consistently readable no matter what language a conversation drifted into.

Before starting a long agentic session, glance at your quota usage — tools like the new macOS menu bar gauge, or a quick check in your account dashboard, can help you decide whether to switch to a lighter model mid-task and avoid hitting rate limits at a critical moment.
